name: rad-session
description:
**Make agent sessions remember each other — even across machines, even across agents (Claude + Codex).** rad-session manages a project's session lifecycle: one-time setup, per-session orientation, per-session capture, automatic git sync. Built around the **canonical doc structure** aligned with published OpenAI/Anthropic project-structure research — the operating manual (CLAUDE.md and/or AGENTS.md per agent scope), `docs/status.md` (evidence-based reality), `docs/planning/current.md` (intent; owned by rad-planner), `docs/planning/archive/` (shipped milestone journal), `docs/decisions/` (ADRs).

What each command does:
- **`/init`** (once per project) — Detects tech stack via pure-Python scripts, asks once which agents are in scope (Claude only / Codex only / both), scaffolds the operating manual's **Operational sections only** (Commands, Compact Instructions, Claude-specific behavior, `@AGENTS.md` import for the shim case) per the sectioned-writer rule, scaffolds `docs/status.md`, creates `.rad/profile` with mode (mentor/dev) and agent_scope, creates `.claude/settings.json` / `.codex/config.toml` as needed, runs a plugin-bloat audit, and recommends running `/rad-planner:plan` if strategic docs are missing. Does NOT write Constitution sections of the operating manual — rad-planner's `/plan` M6 writes those.
- **`/startup`** (every session start) — Fetches origin and prompts to pull when behind. Reads `.rad/profile`, the operating manual per agent_scope, `docs/status.md`, `docs/planning/current.md`. Gap-checks the canonical strategic docs. Surfaces a concise briefing (<35 lines, <5s wall clock — doorman model). Cross-machine continuity surfaced when the last session was on a different host.
- **`/wrapup`** (every session end) — Refuses if no work changed since last status. Writes `docs/status.md` from **evidence** (git diff, test output, plan-task state) — not chat synthesis. Detects candidate decisions mechanically (dep adds, schema changes, env additions, new top-level dirs) + soft triggers. Mode-aware: mentor mode teaches and drafts an ADR; dev mode shows a quick list. Runs cross-doc validators (`doc-redundancy.py` + `doc-contradiction.py` from rad-planner). Prunes the operating manual's **Operational sections only** (sectioned-writer rule). Detects milestone-shipped state (all ACs checked) → proposes archiving `current.md` to `planning/archive/YYYY-MM-DD-MN-slug.md`. Commits + prompts to push. Anomaly-gated final output (silent on success).
- **`/add-resource`** (any time) — Register a new MCP server, CLI tool, script, or note in the operating manual's Resources section.

**Sectioned-writer rule.** The operating manual is the one file with two automated writers. rad-planner owns Constitution sections (Project, Read order, Hard boundaries, Engineering rules, Definition of done, Escalate). rad-session owns Operational sections (Commands, Compact Instructions, Claude-specific behavior, `@AGENTS.md` import line). Each plugin parses sections, modifies only what it owns, and surfaces user-added sections as preserved.

**Cross-machine continuity:** `/wrapup` auto-commits `docs/status.md`, archives, ADRs, and the operating manual; `/startup` fetches and prompts to pull. PC ↔ GitHub ↔ Laptop stays in sync. Flag overrides: `--push` / `--no-push` / `--quick` / `--non-interactive` / `--force` on wrapup.

**Safety:** Never `git add -A`. Never force-pushes. Never merges or rebases automatically. Bails cleanly on diverged history. PreCompact hook fires before context compaction so state isn't silently lost.

Four Python scripts back the lifecycle: `detect-stack.py`, `detect-resources.py`, `audit-plugin-bloat.py`, `migrate-to-v5.py` (one-shot upgrade for projects on the pre-5.0 HANDOFF.md / session-log layout).

Pairs naturally with rad-planner. Works standalone — without rad-planner, `/init` notes missing strategic docs and `/wrapup` gracefully skips cross-doc validators if the scripts aren't present.
